rulings.Case Title: SYLVIA FORSMANN VS. SUTTER HEALTH, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS AN AFFILIATE OF et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 22, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANT HOSPICE BY THE BAY DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled. The fraud/deception allegations in the 12th cause of action relate back. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SYLVIA FORSMANN VS. SUTTER HEALTH, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS AN AFFILIATE OF et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 22, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANT HOSPICE BY THE BAY MOTION TO STRIKE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Motion to strike granted with ten days leave to amend as to claim for attorney fees on the 11th cause of action. Plaintiff to highlight the statute relied upon. Motion denied as to whether plaintiff can recover fees under CCP section 1021.5 which raises a factual issue. Motion granted without leave to amend as to prayer for damages under the CLRA: Plaintiff failed to comply with CC section 1782(a). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PHILIP THOMASCHIMA VS. ESURANCE INCORPORATED, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 22, 2010, line 9, DEFENDANTS ESURANCE, INCORPORATED DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th and 6th causes of action. No reasonable probability that defects can be cured by amendment. Plaintiff fails to plead the requirements of Ins. Code section 11580.2(i) to support breach of contract. Defendant may conduct discovery, and plaintiff has the affirmative duty to commence arbitration proceedings. No bad faith claim survives without breach of contract. Plaintiff may not plead around the absolute bar to relief created by Ins. Code section 11580.2(i) by recasting the 3rd cause of action as one for unfair competition. Conduct described is prohibited by Ins. Code section 790.3. Fraud is not pled with the requisite particularity. No specific acts by defendant supports outrageous conduct for intentional infliction of emotional distress claim. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PHILIP THOMASCHIMA VS. ESURANCE INCORPORATED, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 22, 2010, line 9, DEFENDANT ESURANCE, INCORPORATED Motion to Strike. Motion to strike is granted without leave to amend in all respects except overruled as to Paragraphs 21, 22, 29 and 30. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MATTHEW KAMELAMELA VS. DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES, AN AGENCY OF THE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Of Petition For Writ Of Mandate And Judicial Review Of Administrative Order;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 22, 2010, line 13, PETITIONER MATTHEW KAMELAMELA Hearing of Petition for Writ of Mandate and Judicial Review Of Administrative Order. Petition denied. The record reflects that Officer Rueca had reasonable cause to believe that petitioner was driving under the influence. Petitioner was under arrest at 3:15 a.m. when Officer Rueca read the D8367 admonition to Petitioner. (See paragraph 4) After hearing the admonition, a reasonable person would believe he was in custody. Officer Rueca described how petitioner agreed to the blood test, but afterward became obstreperous and moved away from the phlebotomist, refusing the test. No compelling evidence supports head trauma as a reason for petitioner's actions. =(302/CWW)
